mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production signals a strengthening demand environment that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in payroll employment signals a tightening labor market that compresses wage growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in housing starts signals a weakening supply environment that reduces new capacity and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in weakening supply periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening monetary periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real GDP signals a strengthening demand environment that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, but only when the unemployment rate is below 5%.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods with UNRATE < 5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening supply environment that reduces new capacity and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days, but only when the real GDP growth rate is above 2%.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in weakening supply periods with GDPC1 > 2% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days, but only when the inflation rate is above 2%.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in tightening monetary periods with CPIAUCSL > 2% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in payroll employment signals a weakening labor market that expands wage growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, but only when the real GDP growth rate is below 1%.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in weakening labor market periods with GDPC1 < 1% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
